note: Data gap in GOES-P (GOES-18) due to eclipse season.

This flare is observed near the northeast limb from a region that was unnumbered at the time of the event but has since been numbered as Active Region 14233. There is a very faint CME associated with this flare, only seen in several timestamps of SOHO LASCO C2 coronagraph and not seen in any other coronagraph. The speed of the CME is very low, under 200 km/s, so the CME has not been added to this database.
Simultaneous flaring is observed from Active Region 14227 (S18W13).
Simultaneous flaring is observed from Active Region 14227 (S18W13).
Impulsive flare covered by eclipse in GOES Primary.
Simultaneous flaring/brightening from AR 14236 (N11E35).
Simultaneous brightening/flaring from AR 13232 (N04E41).
Simultaneous brightening/flaring from AR 13232 (N04E41).
AR 14236 rotated off of the Earth-facing disk on 2025-10-08. The flare was observed on-disk in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery.
Concurrent brightening observed near N26W34 with AR 14246 (N22W37) in GOES SUVI 131, but the main brightening is from AR 14248.
There is a simultaneous significant flaring in Active Region 14256 (S16E57) at the time as well.
Occulted flare from behind the NW limb.

This is a short duration flare. There is a concurrent longer duration flare from/behind the NE limb, associated with post-eruptive arcade along the NE limb, which is likely associated with the 2025-11-02T12:23Z CME.
This flare was located on or beyond the SE limb in available GOES SUVI 131 imagery. AR 14276 was numbered on 2025-11-06.

Separate brightening in GOES SUVI 131 observed at AR 14272 (N25E36) during the declining phase of the X1.1 flare from S15E90 on or beyond the SE limb which had its end time at 2025-11-04T22:11Z. Manually calculated end time.
This flare was located on or beyond the SE limb in available GOES SUVI 131 imagery and is likely partially occulted. AR 14276 was numbered on 2025-11-06.
Associated with full halo CME observed in GOES CCOR-1 and rise in GOES >10 MeV protons.
Active Region 14288 was numbered on 2025-11-21.
Impulsive occluded flare from behind the SE limb.
Concurrent brightening around S15E80 with AR 14294. Active Region 14299 was numbered on 2025-12-01 and this entry was updated.

This flare originated from a region near the northeast limb which has since been numbered as Active Region 14299, but was unnumbered at the time of the event. Updating this entry to include the correct Active Region association.

Secondary long-duration brightening following the M2.9 class flare from this same region, as a separate eruption with a separate CME signature. Active Region 14299 was numbered on 2025-12-01 and this entry was updated.
Active Region 14299 was numbered on 2025-12-01 and this entry was updated.
There is some simultaneous flaring from AR 14294 (S15W30), but the brighter flare is attributed to AR 14299.
Close proximity between AR 14294 and 14296. Appears closer to AR 14294.
Concurrent brightening with AR 14294 that spans about 2025-12-08T22:22Z-T22:46Z in GOES SUVI 131 imagery.
Partially occulted flare likely just beyond the limb based on GOES SUVI 284 and STEREO A EUVI imagery.
